- Ent. Verschreint Viederast gi mir gäre spatzeieren,
- fir un allem an dem Müllerdal oder opt Mosel.
- Am Müllerdal ginn et vill flott Spazeierweeër
- an ob der Mosel kënne mir Weif maachen oder mam Schëff fueren.
- Mäi Mann lauscht dat gäre klassesch Musik.
- Mir hunn en Abonnement fir d'Philharmonie
- a ginn op mannst zweemol de Mount e Konzert lauschteren.
- Ent. Verschreint Viederast gi mir gäre spatzeieren,
- For and especially in the Müllerdal or on the Moselle.
- In Müllerdal there are many nice walking places
- and along the Moselle we can do wine tasting or travel by boat.
- My husband likes classical music.
- We have a subscription for the Philharmonie
- and go at least twice a month to listen to a concert.

- Mat de Scouten maache mir vill interessant Aktivitéiten:
- Mir grillen a campéieren dobaussen,
- mir sangen vill a mir spille Theater.
- Heiansdo maache mir och en Ausfluch, zum Beispill op Feierenen oder op Esch-Sauer.
- Freides no der Schoul gi mir oft an de Kino.
- With the scouts we do many interesting activities:
- We grill and camp outside,
- we sing a lot and we perform theater.
- Sometimes we also do an excursion, for example to Feieren or to Esch-Sauer.
- On Fridays after school we often go to the cinema.

- Ech gi gär mat de Kolleege raus.
- Mëttes um zwielef Auer gi mir normalerweis fir den Afterwork an d'Aal Stad.
- Wann d'Wieder gutt ass, ginn ech de Weekend gär klammen.
- Owes ginn ech dacks an d'riichte Klausen.

- At noon at twelve o'clock I normally go afterwork in the Aal Stad.
- If the weather is good, I like to climb on the weekend.

- Ech sinn Manuela Weidartz.
- Ech schaffe bei enger Bank.
- Ech schaffe just hallef Deeg, fir en Haus bis zwielef Auer.
- Mäi Job ass stresseg, mä ganz interessant.
- Ech berode Kliënten a kontrolléieren din Bassissiounen.
- Mäi Meedchent Laura geet moies an de Krësch.
- No mettes hunn ech Zeit fir hat.
- Mir ginn op de Spillplatz oder an d'Schwämm.
- Owes ass ganz Famill zesummen.
- Mäi Mann spillt e bësse mam Laura,
- an dann iessen mir zesummen zu Nuest.
- Dënschdes owes ginn ech an de Fitness,
- a samschdes ginn ech op de Fussballstraining.
- Haians do ginn ech mat menge Mann an de Restaurant oder an de Kino.
- Da passt meng Mama op d'Laura op.
- I am Manuela Weidartz.
- I work at a bank.
- I work only half a day, for a house until twelve o'clock.
- My job is stressful, but very interesting.
- I advise clients and control the deposits.
- My girlfriend Laura goes to bed in the morning.
- In the afternoon I have time for her.
- We go to the playground or to the pool.
- In the evening the whole family is together.
- My husband plays a little with Laura,
- and then we eat together at noon.
- On Tuesdays in the evening I go to the gym,
- and on Saturdays I go to football training.
- On Sundays I go with my husband to the restaurant or to the cinema.
- Then my mom looks after Laura.

- Wéi heescht Dir?
- Mäi Numm ass Sophie.
- Vu wou kommt Dir?
- Ech kommen aus Frankräich.
- An Dir?
- Ech sinn aus Lëtzebuerg.
- Sidd Dir Schwäizer?
- Nee, ech sinn Däitsch.
- Wéi al sidd Dir?
- Ech sinn 25 Joer al.
- Wou wunnt Dir?
- Ech wunnen zu Lëtzebuerg.
- Wat maacht Dir?
- Ech sinn Student.
- A wat studéiert Dir?
- Ech studéieren Geschicht.
- Hutt Dir Geschwëster?
- Jo, ech hunn eng Schwëster.
- Wéi heescht Är Schwëster?
- Si heescht Marie.
- Hutt Dir Hausdéieren?
- Jo, ech hunn eng Kaz.
- Wéi heescht Är Kaz?
- Si heescht Mimi.
- Wat ass Äre Liiblingsiessen?
- Ech iessen gär Pizza.
- An Dir?
- Ech iessen gär Nuddelen.
- Wéi laang léiert Dir schonn Lëtzebuergesch?
- Ech léieren zënter engem Joer.
- Ass et schwéier?
- Jo, et ass schwéier, mä et ass interessant.
- Wat maacht Dir an Ärer Fräizäit?
- Ech liesen gär Bicher.
- A wat maacht Dir nach?
- Ech spillen Gittar.
- Wéi oft spillt Dir Gittar?
- Ech spillen all Dag.
- Wéi laang spillt Dir schonn Gittar?
- Ech spillen zënter dräi Joer.
- Wéi ass d’Wieder haut?
- Et ass sonneg a waarm.
- Gitt Dir gär an d’Vakanz?
- Jo, ech ginn gär an d’Vakanz.
- Wou gitt Dir am léifsten?
- Ech ginn gär an d’Bierger.
- Hutt Dir Pläng fir d’Wochend?
- Jo, ech ginn an de Kino.
- Wat fir e Film kuckt Dir?
- Ech kucken eng Komedie.
- Gutt, vill Spaass!
- Merci! Iech och!
- What is your name?
- My name is Sophie.
- And where are you from?
- I come from France.
- And you?
- I am from Luxembourg.
- Are you Swiss?
- No, I am German.
- How old are you?
- I am 25 years old.
- Where do you live?
- I live in Luxembourg.
- What do you do?
- I am a student.
- And what do you study?
- I study history.
- Do you have siblings?
- Yes, I have a sister.
- What is your sister's name?
- Her name is Marie.
- Do you have pets?
- Yes, I have a cat.
- What is your cat’s name?
- Her name is Mimi.
- What is your favorite food?
- I like to eat pizza.
- And you?
- I like to eat noodles.
- How long have you been learning Luxembourgish?
- I have been learning for one year.
- Is it hard?
- Yes, it’s hard, but it’s interesting.
- What do you do in your free time?
- I like to read books.
- And what else do you do?
- I play guitar.
- How often do you play guitar?

- Den Herr Clemence schafft vu Méindes bis Freides.
- Hie geet normalerweis moies um aacht Auer oder aacht Auer fënnef un.
- An hie fiert owes um fënnef Auer erëm heem.
- Hie mécht net vill Sport, mee hie geet heiansdo do schwammen.
- Seng Fra schafft nëmmen hallefdeeg.
- Méindes, Mëttwochs a Freides moies, an Dënschdes an Donneschdes no Mëtte.
- Si geet oft lafen.
- A si spillt all Samschdes mat enger Frëndin Tennis.
- Mr. Clemence works from Monday to Friday.
- He normally goes in the morning at eight o'clock or eight o'clock five.
- And he celebrates at five o'clock again home.
- He doesn't do much sport, but he goes swimming now and then.
- His wife works only half a day.
- Monday, Wednesday and Friday mornings, and Tuesday and Thursday after noon.
- She goes running often.
- And she plays tennis with a friend every Saturday.

- Gehst du de Weekend mat an de Kino?
- Jo, wat wëlls du kucke goen?
- De neien James Bond.
- Flott, wéini wëlls du goen?
- E Freideschte Nowend.
- E Freidesch?
- Et det ma leed, e Freideschte Nowend hun ech leider keng Zäit.
- Ech geh mat Kollegen iessen.
- Wëlls du dann e Samstëschte Nowend Zäit?
- Jo, dat geet.
- Tiptop, wou leeft de Film?
- Am Utopolis.
- Um wéiviel Auer?
- Um halwer aacht.
- Fuere mir zesummen?
- Ech kommen dës um siwen Auer sichen.
- An da rees, bis Samschdeg.
- Jo, bis dann.
- Salut.
- Do you go to the cinema this weekend?
- Yes, what would you like to watch?
- The new James Bond.
- Great, when would you like to go?
- A Friday night.
- A Friday?
- That’s a pity, on a Friday night I don’t have any time.
- I’m going with colleagues to eat.
- Do you want then a Saturday night time?
- Yes, that works.
- Great, where is the movie playing?
- At Utopolis.
- At what time?
- At half past seven.
- Shall we go together?
- I’m coming at seven o’clock to look for it.
- Then off we go, until Saturday.
- Yes, see you then.
- Bye.

- A nice hello from Barcelona.

- Moie Robert, ech sinn et.
- Moie Mam.
- Kënns du muer de Mëtteg iessen?
- Nee, et deet mer leed, muer hunn ech guer keng Zäit.
- Oh, dat ass awer schued, wat mëss du da muer?
- Ma jo, ech schaffe den ganzen Dag an ech hu keng Mëttepause.
- A muer den Owend?
- Dat geet leider och net.
- No der Aarbecht ginn ech akafen an um siwen Auer ginn ech an den Informatikskurs.
- Du Amen, du hues muer wierklech e grousse Programm.
- Vläicht geet et next Woch?
- Jo, bestëmmt.
- Adee.
- Bis dann, mäi Jong.
- Hello Robert, I am it.
- Hello Mom.
- Can you eat lunch tomorrow?
- No, it hurts me, but I have no time at all.
- Oh, that’s a pity, what do you have tomorrow?
- Well yes, I work all day and I have no lunch break.
- And tomorrow in the evening?
- Unfortunately that also doesn’t work.
- After work I’ll go shopping and at seven o’clock I’m in the computer science course.
- You Amen, you really have a big program tomorrow.
- Maybe next week?
- Yes, definitely.
- Goodbye.
- See you then, my son.

- Moie Robert, ech sinn et.
- Moie Mam.
- Kënns du muer de Mëtteg iessen?
- Nee, et deet mer leed, muer hunn ech guer keng Zäit.
- Oh, dat ass awer schued, wat mëss du da muer?
- Ma jo, ech schaffe den ganzen Dag an ech hu keng Mëttepause.
- A muer den Owend?
- Dat geet leider och net.
- No der Aarbecht ginn ech akafen an um siwen Auer ginn ech an den Informatikskurs.
- Du Amen, du hues muer wierklech e grousse Programm.
- Vläicht geet et next Woch?
- Jo, bestëmmt.
- Adee.
- Bis dann, mäi Jong.

- Hello Robert, I am it.
- Hello Mom.
- Can you eat lunch tomorrow?
- No, it hurts me, but I have no time at all.
- Oh, that’s a pity, what do you have tomorrow?
- Well yes, I work all day and I have no lunch break.
- And tomorrow in the evening?
- Unfortunately that also doesn’t work.
- After work I’ll go shopping and at seven o’clock I’m in the computer science course.
- You Amen, you really have a big program tomorrow.
- Maybe next week?
- Yes, definitely.
- Goodbye.
- See you then, my son.
